primary_legislation.all: Health and Safety at Work Act 1974; Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 2002 (COSHH); L8 ACOP (Legionella: The Control of Legionella Bacteria in Water Systems)

responsible_role: Practice Manager (Responsible Person for water safety / Legionella)
notes: Water safety requirements are identical across nations under L8 ACOP. The only divergence is in the enforcing body that may request records. All steps marked 'all' apply uniformly. Where a GP practice has a hydrotherapy or treatment pool this is clinical equipment outside scope — this logbook covers domestic and utility water systems only.

logbooks.title: Weekly Sentinel Outlet Flushing and Temperature Check
logbooks.frequency_detail: Every 7 days. Sentinel outlets are those at the extremities of the hot and cold water distribution system — least-used taps, showers, sluice outlets.

steps.description: Identify sentinel outlets for the premises from the water risk assessment schematic. In a GP surgery these typically include: least-used patient toilet taps, staff kitchen tap on the furthest run, any shower or hose reel outlet.

Run each cold sentinel outlet for at least 2 minutes. Measure the cold water temperature after 2 minutes. Target: ≤20°C. Record temperature. If temperature exceeds 20°C after flushing, investigate and escalate.

Run each hot sentinel outlet. After 2 minutes, measure the hot water temperature. Target: ≥50°C at outlets (60°C from calorifier). If below 50°C after 2 minutes, escalate to the water treatment contractor.
Record: date, outlet reference, cold temperature measured, hot temperature measured, pass/fail, action taken, tester name.

logbooks.title: Monthly Calorifier (Hot Water Storage) Check
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only where the premises has a hot water storage calorifier or cylinder. Small GP premises with combination boilers may not have this — confirm with the water risk assessment.

steps.description: Check the calorifier flow and return temperatures using the thermometers/gauges fitted to the vessel. Flow: target ≥60°C; return: target ≥50°C.
Confirm the immersion thermostat is set to ≥60°C.
If temperatures are outside target, contact the water treatment or heating contractor immediately. Do not operate the system at sub-target temperatures without professional advice.
Record: date, flow temperature, return temperature, thermostat setting confirmed, action taken, tester name.

logbooks.title: Monthly Cold Water Storage Tank (CWST) Visual Check
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only where the premises has a cold water storage tank (cistern) in the roof space or plant room. Many modern GP premises are on mains-fed supply only.

steps.description: Access the tank location. Confirm the tank lid/cover is in place and undamaged.
Visually inspect the tank: no evidence of contamination, sediment, biofilm, dead animals or insects, discolouration or unusual odour.
Confirm the insulation is intact (preventing heat gain or freezing). Confirm the inlet ball valve is operating correctly (no overflow).
Measure the cold water temperature in the tank. Target: ≤20°C.
Record: date, temperature, visual condition, any issues found, action taken, inspector name.

logbooks.title: Quarterly Thermostatic Mixing Valve (TMV) Blending Check
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only where TMVs are fitted (typically to patient/disabled toilet basins and clinical hand-wash basins to prevent scalding at ≤43°C).

steps.description: Run the outlet fitted with the TMV. After 1 minute, measure the blended outlet temperature. Target: ≤43°C (anti-scald) but must not be so cool as to promote Legionella (should be above 38°C in normal operation).
Confirm the TMV fails safe to cold (check by isolating the cold supply — the outlet should reduce to a trickle or stop, not run hot).
If the blended temperature is outside the 38–43°C range, call the plumbing contractor to adjust or replace the TMV cartridge.
Record: date, TMV reference and location, outlet temperature measured, fail-safe test result, action taken, tester name.

logbooks.title: Quarterly Shower Head Descale and Disinfection
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only where showers are present. Many GP surgeries do not have showers; confirm from site survey.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Every 3 months (or more frequently in hard water areas).

steps.description: Remove the shower head and hose. Dismantle the shower head rose.
Soak the shower head and rose in a descaling solution (proprietary or citric acid) for at least 1 hour. Scrub with a brush to remove all scale and biofilm.
Flush with hot water (≥60°C if possible). Refit the shower head.
Run the shower at full hot for at least 2 minutes to flush and disinfect the hose. Measure outlet temperature — target ≥50°C after 2 minutes.
Record: date, shower reference, descale carried out, flush temperature, any issues, operative name.

logbooks.title: Annual TMV Service and Thermal Flush
logbooks.frequency_detail: Once per year by a competent plumber or water treatment contractor.

steps.description: Isolate the TMV. Remove and inspect the cartridge for fouling, scale or debris.
Clean or replace the cartridge as required. Flush the pipework upstream of the TMV.
Carry out a high-temperature flush (thermal disinfection) of the outlet served by the TMV: bypass or remove the TMV and run the outlet at ≥60°C for at least 1 minute.
Refit the TMV and recommission to the target blended temperature (≤43°C).
Retain the contractor's service report. Record: date, TMVs serviced, any deficiencies, remedial action, next service due.

logbooks.title: Annual Cold Water Storage Tank Inspection and Clean
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only where the premises has a cold water storage tank.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Once per year by a competent water treatment contractor.

steps.description: Arrange with a competent water treatment contractor. Isolate the tank from the mains supply.
Drain the tank completely. Inspect all internal surfaces for scale, sediment, biofilm, corrosion, cracks or contamination. Contractor to photograph findings.
Clean the internal surfaces with an approved disinfectant (chlorine-based, typically 50 mg/l free residual chlorine for 1 hour contact time per BS 8558 / BS EN 806).
Inspect and test the ball valve, overflow, lid, insulation and screen. Replace any defective components.
Refill the tank and confirm chlorine residual is within acceptable levels before returning to service. Flush through to all outlets.
Retain the contractor's report and any water sample results. Record: date, contractor name, condition found, clean carried out, any deficiencies, next inspection due.

logbooks.title: Annual Legionella Risk Assessment Review
logbooks.frequency_detail: At minimum annually, or following any significant change to the water system, building use, or ownership.

steps.description: Retrieve the current Legionella Risk Assessment (LRA). Confirm it was carried out by a competent person and covers the entire water system of the premises (including any branch surgery sites if applicable).

Walk the premises and check for any changes to the water system since the last assessment: new outlets added, pipework alterations, changes to occupancy patterns, any areas of low water use (increased risk).
Review all control measures from the LRA — confirm each is being implemented as specified (flushing records, temperature monitoring, TMV servicing). Review any outstanding actions.
If significant changes have occurred, instruct the competent water treatment specialist to carry out a full reassessment.

Retain the LRA and control measures log. The assessment must be carried out by a competent person as required by L8 ACOP (4th edition). CQC may request evidence of Legionella risk management as part of the 'Safe' domain.
Retain the LRA and control measures log. L8 ACOP applies. HIW may request evidence as part of premises safety.
Retain the LRA and control measures log. L8 ACOP and HSE guidance apply. HIS may request evidence.
Retain the LRA and control measures log. L8 ACOP applies. HSENI and RQIA may request evidence.
Record: date of review, reviewer name and competence, changes identified, actions arising, next review date.
